---
title: "Survival Analysis: Simulation, Cox, Kaplan-Meier, and Bayesian brms"
author: "Statistical Research Workflow"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)

# Ensure required packages are installed
required_pkgs <- c("simsurv", "survival", "survminer", "brms", "tidyverse")
new_pkgs <- required_pkgs[!(required_pkgs %in% installed.packages()[,"Package"])]
if(length(new_pkgs)) install.packages(new_pkgs)

library(simsurv)
library(survival)
library(survminer)
library(brms)
library(tidyverse)

set.seed(42)
```

```
# Create a baseline dataframe of covariates
covariates <- data.frame(
  id = 1:200,
  trt = rbinom(200, 1, 0.5) # 1 = Treatment, 0 = Control
)

# Simulate survival times using a Weibull baseline hazard
# lambdas = scale parameter, gammas = shape parameter
sim_data <- simsurv(
  dist = "weibull",
  lambdas = 0.1,
  gammas = 1.5,
  betas = c(trt = -0.6), # True log hazard ratio
  x = covariates,
  maxt = 5 # Administrative censoring at 5 years
)

# Merge back the covariates and create a standard status indicator
analysis_df <- merge(covariates, sim_data, by = "id") %>%
  mutate(
    status = status, # 1 = event, 0 = censored
    time = eventtime
  )

head(analysis_df)
```

```
cox_fit <- coxph(Surv(time, status) ~ trt, data = analysis_df)
summary(cox_fit)
```

```
km_fit <- survfit(Surv(time, status) ~ trt, data = analysis_df)

ggsurvplot(
  km_fit, 
  data = analysis_df,
  pval = TRUE, 
  conf.int = TRUE,
  risk.table = TRUE,
  legend.labs = c("Control (0)", "Treatment (1)"),
  palette = c("#E41A1C", "#377EB8"),
  title = "Kaplan-Meier Survival Curves"
)
```

```
# Fit the Bayesian model
# We set chains=2 and cores=2 for a faster compile time in this example
brms_fit <- brm(
  formula = time | cens(1 - status) ~ trt, 
  data = analysis_df,
  family = weibull(),
  chains = 2,
  cores = 2,
  iter = 2000,
  seed = 42
)
```

```
summary(brms_fit)
```

```
plot(brms_fit, pars = "b_trt")
```
